visual_checks: MEANING VERIFICATION: Look at each error message visible in the form submission screenshots. Is the error message HELPFUL? Does it tell the user WHAT went wrong and WHERE? 'Please fill out this field' is adequate. 'Error' alone is not. Judge the actual text quality, not just presence.
Submit forms with empty required fields or invalid data — do error messages appear?
Check if error messages identify WHICH field has the error (not just 'There was an error')
Check if error messages DESCRIBE the error (e.g., 'Email must include an @ symbol' rather than just 'Invalid input')
Look for inline validation — are errors shown next to the relevant field?
Check if an error summary at the top of the form lists all errors with links to the problematic fields
Verify that errors are presented in text, not just with color changes or icons alone
Check if error messages are programmatically associated with their fields via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage
pass_conditions: Input errors are automatically detected and described to the user in text
Error messages identify the specific field that has an error
Error messages describe the nature of the error in plain language
Errors are presented in text form — not solely through color, icon, or border changes
Error messages are programmatically associated with their respective form fields (aria-describedby, aria-errormessage, or positioned as part of the label)
Error notifications are announced to screen readers via role='alert', aria-live, or equivalent
fail_conditions: Form submission fails silently with no error message displayed
Error messages are generic and do not identify the problematic field (e.g., 'Please correct errors and try again')
Errors are indicated only by changing the field border to red with no text description
Error messages do not describe what the error is (e.g., 'Invalid' without explaining what is expected)
Error messages exist visually but are not programmatically associated with their form fields — screen readers cannot connect the error to the field
Errors appear visually but are not announced to assistive technology (no aria-live or role='alert')
na_conditions: Page contains no forms or input fields
Page has no input validation — all inputs are free-text with no constraints
No automatic error detection occurs (all validation is server-side without user feedback)
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Forms could not be submitted to trigger validation errors during testing
Error messages are generated by JavaScript that did not execute during testing
Cannot verify error identification without triggering form submission
common_mistakes: Showing only a generic error banner ('Please fix the errors below') without specifying which fields have errors
Indicating errors solely with a red border or background color change on the field
Error messages that say 'Invalid input' without explaining what is expected (e.g., 'Enter a valid email address')
Error icons without accompanying text descriptions
Error messages that appear visually near the field but lack aria-describedby or programmatic association
Client-side validation errors that flash briefly and disappear before the user can read them
Error messages in a language different from the page language

examples.pass: After submitting a registration form with an invalid email, a message appears next to the email field: 'Please enter a valid email address (e.g., name@example.com)'. The message is wrapped in a span with role='alert' and linked to the input via aria-describedby. An error summary at the top lists all errors with links to each field.
examples.fail: A contact form turns three fields red after submission, but no text message explains what is wrong with any of them. Screen reader users receive no notification that errors occurred.
examples.partial: Error messages appear next to each invalid field with descriptive text, but they are not programmatically associated with the fields (no aria-describedby) and no aria-live region announces the errors, so screen reader users must manually find the error messages.

plain_meaning: When the system automatically detects an input error (required field missing, format invalid, value out of range, etc.), it must identify which field is in error and describe the error in text. The description has to be perceivable as text, not only a red border or an icon with no text.
scope_applies_to: Forms that perform client-side or server-side validation
Inline validation errors that appear on blur or keystroke
Error summaries presented at the top of a form
Error messages associated with individual fields
scope_does_not_apply_to: Requiring the system to detect errors at all (that is a functional requirement, not accessibility)
Errors visible only via color, icon, or border with no text description
Error prevention rules (those are SC 3.3.4 and 3.3.6)
Pages with no forms or user input
borderline_calibration: If errors ARE detected but only a red border is shown, FAIL.
If a text message is provided AND the field in error is identifiable (by proximity, by label reference, or programmatic association), PASS.
Do not flag a site for not validating at all (that is out of scope).
Error summaries and per-field messages both count as identification.
Prefer Not Enough Evidence when no error state was captured.
